entityIds.mergedDeviceLinkRule: When DeviceInfo populates a row, append HardwareUuid, AzureVmId, AwsResourceName, GcpFullResourceName to EntityIds; all MergedDeviceIds[*] get appended as { type: MdeDeviceId, id: <predecessorId>, relation: merged_predecessor } so historical Sentinel queries still resolve the asset
entityIds.crossEngineAzureRule: When device is an Azure VM, the same asset has rows in BOTH Endpoint_Profile_CL and Azure_Profile_CL linked via shared EntityIds[?type=='AzureResourceId']

hashes.name: CollectHash
hashes.description: Hash of every phase=in field. Detects upstream drift in raw collected data.

hashes.name: ProfileHash
hashes.description: Hash of every phase=profile field plus rule-set hash. Lets KQL show only verdict-changing snapshots.

mde._policy: include-only (default-deny). Only paths listed here flow to Properties.collect.mde. New MS fields are NOT auto-included -- monthly schema-drift detection adds them after review.

exposureGraph._policy: include-only (default-deny). Monthly schema-drift detection adds new MS fields after review.

deviceInfo._policy: include-only (default-deny). Bulk-aggregated from Defender XDR Advanced Hunting DeviceInfo table; one query per snapshot, NOT per device.

azure._policy: include-only (default-deny). Only present when device is Azure-hosted (microsoft.compute/virtualmachines).

contributors._comment: Asset class matched via TVM/EG/name-pattern -- catalog rules emit SIRules entries; engine MIN-reduces the Tier field.
Locked + custom posture rules emitting TargetTier (DC EG signal, Exchange TVM, etc.).

EG criticalityLevel.criticalityLevel (manual or rule-based) -- weighted hint, not authoritative. Lower than catalog/posture-rule because EG rules can be tenant-customised in ways the operator may not have validated.

MIN tier across top-5 most-frequent logon users in last 3d (cross-engine join with Identity_Profile_CL). Receptionist PC -> no privileged user -> null (no opinion). Finance/HR person's laptop -> Tier 2. IT admin's PAW -> Tier 1. Domain admin desktop -> Tier 0. High weight (0.9) because frequent + identified user is a strong signal, but not 1.0 -- contractor / break-glass / one-off support sessions can pollute the top-5.
Internet-reachable endpoint -- cap at T1 regardless of catalog finding.
Cross-engine join: device used by T0/T1 identity -> escalate device tier.

derivation.algorithm: alias of Hostname (preferred) or DisplayName -- shared cross-engine asset-name field for consistent reporting / dashboards
alias of PrimaryEntityId (= MDE DeviceId / AAD DeviceId) -- shared cross-engine asset-id field for consistent reporting / dashboards

fields.name: MergedDeviceIds
fields.sourcePath: DeviceInfo.MergedDeviceIds (kept in EntityIds with relation: merged_predecessor)

derivation.algorithm: shortcut: services contain NTDS or Netlogon

fields.name: MsCriticalityLevel
fields.sourcePath: eg.node.NodeProperties.rawData.criticalityLevel (Microsoft Exposure Mgmt asset criticality verdict: Low/Medium/High/VeryHigh) -- comparison column only; does NOT drive Tier (per feedback_si_ra_tier_source memory: Tier sources from CL only)

fields.name: MachineRiskState
fields.sourcePath: eg.node.NodeProperties.rawData.machineRiskState (Defender's per-device risk verdict: Low/Medium/High/Informational) -- feeds RiskFactor_Probability_Detailed.HighMachineRisk token

fields.name: IsCompromisedRecently
fields.sourcePath: eg.node.NodeProperties.rawData.isCompromisedRecently (post-compromise indicators within last N days). STRONG signal -- feeds RiskFactor_Probability_Detailed.IsCompromisedRecently token

derivation.shape: [ { Upn, Tier, LogonCount }, ... ] sorted by LogonCount desc, top 5

fields.name: RuleBasedCriticalityLevel
fields.sourcePath: eg.node.NodeProperties.rawData.criticalityLevel.ruleBasedCriticalityLevel (tier from EG rule engine, before manual override)

fields.name: HasAuthorityMisConfigurations
fields.sourcePath: eg.node.NodeProperties.rawData.hasAuthorityMisConfigurations[*] (OS-level / authority misconfigs distinct from Guard misconfigs)

fields.name: DiscoverySourceProducts
fields.sourcePath: eg.node.NodeProperties.rawData.discoverySources-udi.sources[*].productName (Defender for Endpoint, MDI, Azure Arc, etc.)

derivation.algorithm: distinct ports where direction:Inbound, access:Allow, sourceCidr contains 0.0.0.0/0
distinct ports where sourceCidr is public CIDR but not 0.0.0.0/0
port 22/3389/5985/5986 with sourceCidr = single specific external IP
[{port, service, sourceType, severity, ruleId, priority}] per open port
distinct destinationCidr/port pairs for Outbound Allow rules

fields.name: AssetTags
fields.sourcePath: derived from MachineTags + DeviceManualTags + DeviceDynamicTags (deduped, joined by ';')
fields._comment: Concatenation of MachineTags + DeviceManualTags + DeviceDynamicTags joined with ';'. Used by RA __EXCLUDED_ASSET_TAGS__ filter to drop devices the operator has explicitly tagged as excluded (e.g. '--Excluded--SI'). Empty string when no tags. Computed in Build-EndpointProfileRow.ps1 so RA queries can run a cheap `where AssetTags has_any (_excludedAssetTags)` without per-row JSON expansion.

derivation.see: engine/shared/Get-SIRiskFactors.ps1 -- legacy/EOL OS list (Windows 7/8/8.1/Server 2003-2012, EOL Linux distros, macOS major < 13)
engine/shared/Get-SIRiskFactors.ps1 -- true when any EG manual/dynamic/tags array contains '--Excluded--SI'. Operator-driven opt-out from RA reports.

inputs: max of LastSignInDateTime, LastSeen, EgLastSeen, MDE_LastSeen, EG_LastSeen

fields.name: cmdbId
fields.sourcePath: cmdb.id (CMDB row primary key when matched at Reconcile)

derivation.algorithm: Reconcile-stage classification of how the asset was matched to CMDB: rule / identity / orphan / stale-cache / gap-cis
Reconcile-stage match outcome: matched / unmatched / ambiguous
